text.title: nosql-cassandra-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for nosql-cassandra-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Hot partition: using a single low-cardinality value as the partition key (e.g., status='active', region='us-east', or a single tenant_id for a multi-tenant table).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: All writes for that value route to the same replica set. One node becomes a bottleneck while the rest of the cluster is idle. Symptoms: p99 write latency spikes, compaction backlog, eventual timeout errors on that node.

failure-mode.description: Using a raw timestamp or auto-increment ID as the sole partition key.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Monotonically increasing keys map to sequential token ranges. All recent writes hit the same node (the one owning the current token range). This is called a "write hotspot" and is a classic Cassandra deployment failure mode.
